headline: AI Stocks Are Rallying on Iran Peace Deal News, but the Risks Just Got a Lot Bigger
summary: The stock market was overlooking Anthropic’s latest feud with the U.S. government on Monday, which highlights what could be an ongoing problem for tech stocks: government intervention.  Anthropic announced on Friday that it had suspended all access to its newest and most capable models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.  This came after the government issued an export control directive to suspend access to the models by any foreign national, citing national security concerns.

headline: Canada's Nuvei to buy Payoneer for $2.75 billion in cross-border payments push
summary: Nuvei will buy cross-border payments firm Payoneer for about $2.75 billion in cash, the companies said on Monday, as ‌the Canadian fintech aims to expand globally.  Nuvei will buy all ‌shares of Payoneer for $7.40 apiece, a premium of about 44% to their last close ​on June 8.  Payoneer has a market capitalization of about $2.26 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

headline: Fox to buy streaming pioneer Roku in a $22 billion deal
summary: Fox Corp. has agreed to buy the streaming pioneer Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion, including debt.  Roku will continue to be run as an open, partner-friendly platform, the companies said Monday, and there appears to be no immediate changes that customers will see.  Fox and Roku said that the combined company will become the third-largest player in U.S. television by share of viewing.

raw.headline: Fox to buy streaming pioneer Roku in a $22 billion deal

Roku will continue to be run as an open, partner-friendly platform, the companies said Monday, and there appears to be no immediate changes that customers will see.  Fox and Roku said that the combined company will become the third-largest player in U.S. television by share of viewing.

headline: Musk says SpaceX could bring $1 trillion in revenue by 2030
summary: Elon Musk said on Sunday that his rocket company, SpaceX, ‌could bring in $1 trillion in revenue ‌by 2030, making the statement two days after the ​company went public, valuing it at over $2 trillion.  "And I would be surprised if revenue is not greater than $1T in 2031," he ‌wrote on his ⁠social media platform X, replying to journalist and financial commentator Jon Erlichman.  SpaceX ⁠on Friday became the sixth-largest U.S. firm, cementing Musk's status as the world's first ​trillionaire.
